MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2014 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. PARK JENNINGS, TEL.NO. 788-7600 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. PATRICK E. TAYLOR OF THE BREMER WHYTE, TEL. NO. (510)540-4881 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS KEVIN WALLACE AND WALLACE REMODELING INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE COMPLAINT FOR MONETARY DAMAGES, PUNITIVE DAMAGES, EQUITABLE AND DECLARATORY RELIEF is denied without prejudice. Defendants failed to comply with California Rule of Court 3.1322 requiring a notice of motion to strike a portion of the pleading to quote the full portion sought to be stricken except where the motion is to strike an entire paragraph or a complete cause of action. Defendants' Motion sought both selected portions and entire paragraphs of the Complaint be struck. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-30-2014 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. PARK JENNINGS, TEL.NO. 788-7600 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. PATRICK E. TAYLOR OF THE BREMER WHYTE, TEL. NO. (510)540-4881 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANTS.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS NOT ARGUED. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS KEVIN WALLACE AND WALLACE REMODELING INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is overruled. As to Causes of Action One, Three, Five, and Eleven, the Complaint states facts sufficient to constitute causes of action against Defendants Kevin Wallace and Wallace Remodeling, Inc. C.C.P. 430.10(e). The Complaint adequately alleges the statutory elements of these causes of action. The Complaint also adequately pleads who, what, when, and how the alleged misrepresentations were made. The Demurrer to the Sixth and Seventh Causes of Action is overruled because the Complaint sufficiently alleges facts sufficient to constitute causes of action for Professional Negligence and Negligent/Defective Construction. Defendants Kevin Wallace and Wallace Remodeling, Inc.'s Demurrers to the Complaint on the ground of uncertainty are overruled. C.C.P. 430.10(f). Defendants have not addressed in their memorandum of points and authorities how the First, Third, Fifth, and Eleventh Causes of Action are uncertain. The Sixth Cause of Action alleges sufficient facts to constitute a cause of action and is not uncertain. The title of the Seventh Cause of Action does not make that cause of action uncertain. See Williams v. Beechnut Nutrition Corp. (1986) 185 Cal.App.3d 135, 139, f.n. 2. The Eleventh Cause of Action states a cause of action and a demurrer cannot attack the amount of damages requested in a complaint. See Venice Town Council, Inc. v. City of Los Angeles (1996) 47 Cal.App.4th 1547, 1561-62. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976
